Client 2: And I’m Mary. I work a regular job, and I’m the one who likes the spreadsheet version of reality—income, expenses, insurance, all of it.
Advisor: Perfect. We’ll keep it practical. Let’s start with income at the household level—roughly what’s your total gross income in a year right now?
Client 1: All-in, household gross is 146250 annually.
Advisor: Got it—thank you. And Daniel, just so I’m not mixing streams, your self-employed gross is what per year?
Client 1: My gross is 106,600 a year—give or take, but that’s the number.

Client 1: Roughly forty-five hundred a month.
Advisor: Okay—when you say forty-five hundred, is there a more exact number you want me to use for planning?
Client 2: Yes—use 4550 per month. That’s our total monthly expenses number.
Advisor: Perfect. Now debt—student loans, car loans, credit cards you’re carrying month to month—what are the total monthly debt payments across everything?
Client 1: Zero. Monthly debt payments total is 0.

Client 2: And I’m Mary. I work a regular job, and I’m the one who likes the spreadsheet version of reality—income, expenses, insurance, all of it.

Client 1: My gross is 106,600 a year—give or take, but that’s the number.

Client 2: Correct—no loans outstanding.
Advisor: Great. Let’s talk assets next—investable accounts first, then the house. About how much do you have in investable assets total?
Client 1: Total investable assets is 509350.
Advisor: Thanks. Now I’m going to break that into the buckets you actually see on statements. Do you have a joint taxable account—like a brokerage or taxable savings—what’s the value there?
Client 2: We have a joint taxable account at a bank, and the value is 263,350.

Client 2: Yes, in my name—private markets—79,650, and it’s held at a bank.
Advisor: Great. And then the home—what are you using as the current value of your primary residence?
Client 2: 393,850 for the primary residence value.
Advisor: Thanks. Now protection—Daniel, do you have life insurance in place right now?
Client 1: Yes, I do.

Client 2: Correct.
Advisor: Perfect—thanks. Since the debt side is clean, I want to zoom out to a couple of planning assumptions. First, where are you two filing from—what state are you resident in?
Client 2: Texas—TX.
Advisor: Got it, thanks Mary. And on taxes—when you think about your marginal bracket, where do you land most years?
Client 1: We’re in the 37% bracket.

Client 2: Texas—TX.
Advisor: Got it, thanks Mary. And on taxes—when you think about your marginal bracket, where do you land most years?
Client 1: We’re in the 37% bracket.
Advisor: Okay. And Daniel, when you say 37%, you mean the federal bracket band you plan around—right?
Client 2: Yes—37% is the band we use for planning.

Advisor: Yep, that makes sense. Mary, same page there?
Client 2: Yes—growth.
Advisor: Okay. And then just so you know how I work with you going forward—are you looking for this to be an ongoing advisory relationship, like an RIA, or more of a one-time plan?
Client 1: Ongoing. RIA (registered investment advisor).
Advisor: Got it. And Mary, same expectation—ongoing, not just a one-off?

Advisor: Okay. And Daniel, when you say 37%, you mean the federal bracket band you plan around—right?
Client 2: Yes—37% is the band we use for planning.
Advisor: Great. Next one is more about how we invest. If you had to pick a simple label for your comfort with market ups and downs—conservative, moderate, growth—where do you two sit?
Client 1: Growth. I mean—growth, but not reckless.
Advisor: Yep, that makes sense. Mary, same page there?

Advisor: Hi Daniel, hi Mary—good to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into numbers, what prompted you to reach out now?
Client 1: Yeah—so I’m Daniel. I’m self-employed, sales, and I just want to make sure we’re not missing anything big, especially with the kids and all.
Client 2: And I’m Mary. I work a regular job, and I’m the one who likes the spreadsheet version of reality—income, expenses, insurance, all of it.
Advisor: Perfect. We’ll keep it practical. Let’s start with income at the household level—roughly what’s your total gross income in a year right now?

Client 2: Yes, married.
Advisor: Okay. Next—dependents. How many people are you supporting besides the two of you?
